description: Equipment-type level hazard catalog. Each equipmentType entry contains a curated, selectable-only hazard list with structured options, contextual tags, quick defaults, ruleId, and standards.

hazards.title: Sterility failure due to load cold spots
hazards.definition: Insufficient cycle penetration in worst-case load locations causing inadequate microbial inactivation.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Nonsterile product released causing patient harm.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Occurs often under current loading practices.
probabilityOptions.example: Repeated cold spots observed in mapping.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for essentially every operation or load.
exposureOptions.example: All production loads are terminally sterilized.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for most loads or cycles.
exposureOptions.example: Majority of batches use dense trays.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for some loads or product types.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present only in exceptional cases.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Very likely to be detected before harm via real-time monitoring or biological indicators.
detectabilityOptions.example: Continuous BI monitoring and chamber profiling.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Likely to be detected by routine checks or periodic mapping.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Unlikely to be detected before harm without targeted mapping.
detectabilityOptions.example: No routine mapping at worst-case locations.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No practical detection mechanism exists before harm.
detectabilityOptions.example: No biological indicators or mapping performed.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated cycles, load maps, and BI program substantially reduce risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Validated worst-case loads with routine BIs.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but some residual uncertainty remains.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but are manual or inconsistently applied.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls are absent or do not reduce the hazard meaningfully.

hazards.title: Air removal failure in pre-vacuum cycle
hazards.definition: Incomplete air removal causing cold pockets and ineffective sterilization.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Air removal failure leads to nonsterile product release.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Air removal failure causes lot rejection.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Air removal failure detected by Bowie-Dick and real-time sensors.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Detected by periodic tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: May go unnoticed without targeted tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated pre-vacuum cycles and monitoring minimize risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but occasional gaps exist.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Cycle parameter drift due to sensor failure
hazards.definition: Sensor drift or failure causes temperature or pressure to move out of specification without immediate detection.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Drift causes patient harm or unsafe release.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Drift causes lot rejection or recall.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.example: Known sensor drift requiring frequent recalibration.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Drift detected by automated trending and alarms.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Detected by periodic profiling.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: May go unnoticed without targeted tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Redundant sensors and automatic calibration minimize drift.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Regular calibration and maintenance reduce drift risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but are manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No meaningful controls.

hazards.title: Residual sterilant or toxic residues
hazards.definition: Residual sterilant or byproduct remains on product above acceptable limits after aeration.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause severe patient harm or death.
severityOptions.example: High EtO residuals on implantable devices.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause serious adverse effects or recalls.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause rework or additional processing.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.help: Minor nonclinical effects or nuisance odors.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals occur often under current process.

probabilityOptions.label: Occasional
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals occur several times per year.
probabilityOptions.example: Occasional aeration failures for dense loads.

probabilityOptions.label: Remote
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals are unlikely with current controls.
probabilityOptions.example: Validated aeration and monitoring in place.

probabilityOptions.label: Extremely remote
probabilityOptions.help: Very unlikely given robust aeration and controls.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All loads are at risk until controlled.
exposureOptions.example: All products processed with EtO or similar.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Most loads may be exposed to residuals.
exposureOptions.example: Many dense loads requiring long aeration.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Some loads or product types are exposed.
exposureOptions.example: Only porous items show residual issues.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Residuals occur only in exceptional cases.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Residuals are routinely tested and detected before release.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic testing detects residuals in sampling.
detectabilityOptions.example: Batch sampling for residuals performed monthly.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Residuals are not routinely tested and may go unnoticed.
detectabilityOptions.example: No routine residual testing for some products.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism for residuals exists.
detectabilityOptions.example: No aeration or residual testing performed.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated aeration and monitoring reliably remove residuals.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Validated aeration cycles with residual testing.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce residuals but occasional issues occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but are inconsistent or manual.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Manual aeration steps with operator variability.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls to remove residuals.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: No aeration or monitoring in place.

hazards.title: Operator thermal exposure and burn
hazards.definition: Operator or maintenance personnel exposed to high temperatures or steam causing injury.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Exposure could cause death or life-threatening injury.
severityOptions.example: Severe burn from unexpected steam release.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.example: Second degree burns from hot surfaces.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.help: Minor discomfort or no treatment required.

severityOptions.label: Negligible
severityOptions.example: No operator exposure in normal operation.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Injuries occur often under current procedures.
probabilityOptions.example: Repeated operator contact with hot surfaces.

probabilityOptions.label: Occasional
probabilityOptions.help: Injuries occur several times per year.

probabilityOptions.label: Remote
probabilityOptions.help: Injuries are unlikely with current controls.
probabilityOptions.example: Proper PPE and guards reduce incidents.

probabilityOptions.label: Extremely remote
probabilityOptions.help: Very unlikely given robust safety measures.
probabilityOptions.example: Interlocks and safety procedures prevent exposure.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: Operators are exposed during most operations.
exposureOptions.example: Frequent manual loading/unloading with hot surfaces.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.example: Maintenance tasks require brief access to hot zones.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Safety systems and training make incidents likely to be prevented or detected.
detectabilityOptions.example: Interlocks and PPE prevent most exposures.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Incidents detected by routine supervision or incident reporting.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Incidents may go unreported or unnoticed.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection or prevention measures exist.
detectabilityOptions.example: No PPE or safety training provided.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Engineering controls and interlocks prevent exposure reliably.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Automatic door interlocks and insulated surfaces.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Administrative controls and PPE reduce risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: SOPs and PPE in place with training.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but rely on human behavior.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: PPE available but not consistently used.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls to prevent thermal exposure.

hazards.title: Sterility failure due to inadequate steam penetration
hazards.definition: Steam penetration insufficient for dense or porous loads causing inadequate microbial inactivation.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Nonsterile product released causing patient harm.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Occurs often under current loading practices.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for essentially every operation or load.
exposureOptions.example: All production loads are terminally sterilized.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for most loads or cycles.
exposureOptions.example: Majority of batches use dense trays.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present for some loads or product types.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Hazard present only in exceptional cases.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Very likely to be detected before harm via biological indicators and mapping.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Likely to be detected by routine checks or periodic mapping.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Unlikely to be detected before harm without targeted mapping.
detectabilityOptions.example: No routine mapping at worst-case locations.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No practical detection mechanism exists before harm.
detectabilityOptions.example: No biological indicators or mapping performed.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated cycles, load maps, and BI program substantially reduce risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Validated worst-case loads with routine BIs.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but some residual uncertainty remains.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but are manual or inconsistently applied.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls are absent or do not reduce the hazard meaningfully.

hazards.title: Cycle parameter drift due to steam quality
hazards.definition: Steam quality variations (wet steam, condensate) cause cycle parameter deviations.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Drift causes lot rejection or rework.
severityOptions.example: Multiple batches affected by wet steam.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Steam quality monitored and alarms present.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Detected by periodic checks.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: May go unnoticed without targeted tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Steam treatment and monitoring minimize risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Steam dryers and traps with monitoring.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but occasional issues occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Residual EtO toxic residues
hazards.definition: EtO residuals remain on product above acceptable limits after aeration.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause severe patient harm or death.
severityOptions.example: High EtO residuals on implantable devices.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause serious adverse effects or recalls.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Residuals cause rework or additional processing.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.help: Minor nonclinical effects or nuisance odors.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals occur often under current process.

probabilityOptions.label: Occasional
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals occur several times per year.
probabilityOptions.example: Occasional aeration failures for dense loads.

probabilityOptions.label: Remote
probabilityOptions.help: Residuals are unlikely with current controls.
probabilityOptions.example: Validated aeration and monitoring in place.

probabilityOptions.label: Extremely remote
probabilityOptions.help: Very unlikely given robust aeration and controls.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All loads are at risk until controlled.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Most loads may be exposed to residuals.
exposureOptions.example: Many dense loads requiring long aeration.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Some loads or product types are exposed.
exposureOptions.example: Only porous items show residual issues.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Residuals occur only in exceptional cases.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Residuals are routinely tested and detected before release.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic testing detects residuals in sampling.
detectabilityOptions.example: Batch sampling for residuals performed monthly.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Residuals are not routinely tested and may go unnoticed.
detectabilityOptions.example: No routine residual testing for some products.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism for residuals exists.
detectabilityOptions.example: No aeration or residual testing performed.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated aeration and monitoring reliably remove residuals.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Validated aeration cycles with residual testing.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce residuals but occasional issues occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls exist but are inconsistent or manual.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Manual aeration steps with operator variability.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls to remove residuals.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: No aeration or monitoring in place.

hazards.title: EtO cycle parameter deviation
hazards.definition: EtO concentration, humidity, or dwell time out of specification causing underprocessing.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.help: Deviations occur often under current controls.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Real-time monitoring detects deviations quickly.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Detected by periodic checks.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: May go unnoticed without targeted tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated EtO control and monitoring minimize risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Automated control with alarms and redundancy.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but occasional issues occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Temperature excursion
hazards.definition: Stored product experiences temperature outside validated limits.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Temperature excursion causes patient harm or product unusable.
severityOptions.example: Biologic potency lost causing patient risk.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Excursion causes lot rejection or major rework.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Excursion causes rework or limited scrap.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.help: Minor quality impact manageable by rework.

severityOptions.label: Negligible
severityOptions.example: Transient logging spike with no effect.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All stored product exposed until corrected.
exposureOptions.example: Entire storage used for temperature-sensitive product.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Real-time monitoring and alarms detect excursions quickly.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect many excursions.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Excursions may go unnoticed without sampling.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism present.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Redundant refrigeration and alarms minimize risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but some gaps exist.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Manual checks only during business hours.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls in place.

hazards.title: Loss of refrigeration
hazards.definition: Complete loss of cooling leading to prolonged out-of-spec conditions.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Loss causes severe patient risk or total product loss.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Significant product loss or recall risk.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.example: Short outage with no product loss.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All stored product exposed during outage.
exposureOptions.example: Single storage area for all product.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.example: Primary storage used for many products.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Alarms and remote monitoring detect loss immediately.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect outages.
detectabilityOptions.example: Daily checks reveal outage next day.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Outage may go unnoticed for some time.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Backup power and redundant refrigeration mitigate loss.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Some mitigation exists but single points remain.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Manual transfer to backup units possible.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Mitigation limited or slow to enact.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No mitigation available.

hazards.title: Seal failure
hazards.definition: Package seal integrity compromised causing contamination or moisture ingress.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Seal failure leads to patient harm or sterile breach.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Seal failure causes lot rejection or recall.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Seal failure causes rework or scrap.

severityOptions.label: Negligible
severityOptions.example: Cosmetic seal imperfection not affecting sterility.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All packages at risk until fixed.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: In-line inspection detects seal failures reliably.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect many failures.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Failures may go unnoticed until downstream.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Automated sealing and inspection minimize failures.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce failures but occasional defects occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Foreign material inclusion
hazards.definition: Foreign object introduced into package or product during packaging.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Foreign material causes severe patient harm.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Foreign material causes recall or major rework.
severityOptions.example: Metal fragment found in multiple packages.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Foreign material causes rework or scrap.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All packages at risk until fixed.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Metal detectors or vision systems detect inclusions reliably.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Sampling detects many inclusions.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Inclusions may go unnoticed without testing.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Multiple detection and housekeeping controls minimize risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but some gaps exist.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Incorrect calibration
hazards.definition: Calibration out of tolerance leading to biased measurements.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Calibration error causes patient harm or release of unsafe product.
severityOptions.example: Balance error causes incorrect dose release.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Calibration error causes lot rejection or recall.
severityOptions.example: CMM error leads to dimensional nonconformance.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Calibration error causes rework or scrap.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.example: No calibration schedule leading to frequent drift.

probabilityOptions.label: Remote
probabilityOptions.example: Regular calibration with low failure rate.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.example: Primary measurement device used for all releases.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.example: Multiple instruments share same calibration issue.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Calibration errors detected by routine checks and controls.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect many issues.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Errors may go unnoticed without targeted tests.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Robust calibration program and traceability minimize risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but occasional lapses occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective calibration controls.

hazards.title: Incorrect assay result
hazards.definition: Analytical method yields false result affecting release.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Incorrect result causes patient harm or unsafe release.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Incorrect result causes recall or major rework.
severityOptions.example: Assay bias leading to lot rejection.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Incorrect result causes rework or additional testing.

severityOptions.label: Negligible
severityOptions.example: Noncritical QC parameter out of range.

probabilityOptions.label: Frequent
probabilityOptions.example: Poor method control causing repeated failures.

probabilityOptions.label: Remote
probabilityOptions.example: Validated method with low failure rate.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All release decisions rely on the assay.
exposureOptions.example: Single assay used for product release.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Most releases rely on the assay.
exposureOptions.example: Assay used for majority of batches.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Some releases rely on the assay.
exposureOptions.example: Assay used for specific product lines.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Only occasional reliance on the assay.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: System suitability and controls detect errors before release.
detectabilityOptions.example: System suitability checks run each batch.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect many issues.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Errors may go unnoticed without targeted checks.
detectabilityOptions.example: No system suitability for some methods.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Validated method, calibration, and system suitability minimize risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Method validation and routine system suitability.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but occasional issues occur.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

hazards.title: Data integrity failure in chromatography data system
hazards.definition: Loss or alteration of analytical data affecting release decisions.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: Data loss leads to unsafe release or patient harm.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: Data integrity issues cause recall or regulatory action.
severityOptions.example: Altered audit trails discovered post release.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: Data issues cause rework or additional verification.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All runs rely on the affected data system.
exposureOptions.example: CDS used for all release decisions.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.help: Most runs rely on the system.

exposureOptions.label: Intermittent
exposureOptions.help: Some runs rely on the system.

exposureOptions.label: Rare
exposureOptions.help: Only occasional reliance on the system.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Audit trails and checks detect integrity issues quickly.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic review detects many issues.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Issues may go unnoticed without targeted review.
detectabilityOptions.example: No routine audit of audit trails.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: CSV and controls minimize data integrity risk.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Validated CDS with immutable audit trail.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but some gaps exist.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Partially effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls inconsistent or manual.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Ineffective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: No effective controls.

equipmentTypes.equipmentType: HVAC Air Handling Unit serving cleanrooms

hazards.title: HVAC failure causing particle or pressure excursions
hazards.definition: HVAC loss causes particle or pressure excursions in controlled areas leading to contamination risk.

severityOptions.label: Catastrophic
severityOptions.help: HVAC failure causes sterility loss or patient harm.
severityOptions.example: Loss of pressure cascade in aseptic suite causing contamination.

severityOptions.label: Major
severityOptions.help: HVAC failure causes lot rejection or major rework.

severityOptions.label: Moderate
severityOptions.help: HVAC failure causes rework or downtime.

severityOptions.label: Minor
severityOptions.example: Short recovery time with no product impact.

exposureOptions.label: Continuous
exposureOptions.help: All production in affected zones exposed.
exposureOptions.example: Single HVAC serves multiple sterile zones.

exposureOptions.label: Frequent
exposureOptions.example: Primary HVAC serves majority of operations.

detectabilityOptions.label: High detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Environmental monitoring systems detect HVAC issues quickly.
detectabilityOptions.example: Continuous particle and pressure monitoring with alerts.

detectabilityOptions.label: Moderate detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Periodic checks detect many issues.

detectabilityOptions.label: Low detectability
detectabilityOptions.help: Issues may go unnoticed without monitoring.

detectabilityOptions.label: None
detectabilityOptions.help: No detection mechanism.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Very effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Redundancy and monitoring minimize HVAC risk.

controlEffectivenessOptions.label: Effective
controlEffectivenessOptions.help: Controls reduce risk but single points remain.
controlEffectivenessOptions.example: Backup units available but manual switchover.
